formulas.name: Domain and Range
formulas.variables: R - set of real numbers, f(x) - function
formulas.notes: The set of all possible input values for which the function is defined.

formulas.name: Range
formulas.variables: f(x) - function, Domain - domain of the function
formulas.notes: The set of all possible output values of the function.

formulas.name: Codomain
formulas.formula: Codomain: set of all possible output values
formulas.variables: set of all possible output values
formulas.notes: The set of all possible output values of the function, which may not be the same as the range.

formulas.name: Function Composition
formulas.variables: f - function, g - function, x - input value
formulas.notes: The composition of two functions f and g, denoted by (f ∘ g)(x), is defined as f(g(x)).

formulas.name: Inverse Function
formulas.variables: f - function, x - input value, y - output value
formulas.notes: A function f is invertible if and only if it is one-to-one (injective).

formulas.name: One-to-One (Injective) Function
formulas.variables: f - function, x1 - input value, x2 - input value
formulas.notes: A function f is one-to-one if and only if f(x1) = f(x2) implies x1 = x2.

formulas.name: Onto (Surjective) Function
formulas.formula: ∀y ∈ codomain, ∃x ∈ domain such that f(x) = y
formulas.variables: f - function, y - output value, x - input value
formulas.notes: A function f is onto if and only if for every y in the codomain, there exists an x in the domain such that f(x) = y.

formulas.name: Bijective Function
formulas.formula: f is both one-to-one and onto
formulas.notes: A function f is bijective if and only if it is both one-to-one and onto.

formulas.name: Equivalence Relation
formulas.formula: A relation R on a set A is an equivalence relation if and only if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive
formulas.notes: A relation R on a set A is an equivalence relation if and only if it satisfies the following properties: (1) R is reflexive, (2) R is symmetric, and (3) R is transitive.

formulas.name: Reflexive Relation
formulas.variables: R - relation, A - set, x - element of the set
formulas.notes: A relation R on a set A is reflexive if and only if for every element x in A, x is related to x, i.e., xRx.

formulas.name: Symmetric Relation
formulas.variables: R - relation, A - set, x - element of the set, y - element of the set
formulas.notes: A relation R on a set A is symmetric if and only if for every pair of elements x and y in A, if x is related to y, then y is also related to x, i.e., xRy ⇒ yRx.

formulas.name: Transitive Relation
formulas.formula: ∀x, y, z ∈ A, xRy ∧ yRz ⇒ xRz
formulas.variables: R - relation, A - set, x - element of the set, y - element of the set, z - element of the set
formulas.notes: A relation R on a set A is transitive if and only if for every triple of elements x, y, and z in A, if x is related to y and y is related to z, then x is also related to z, i.e., xRy ∧ yRz ⇒ xRz.
